title: echinochloa crus-galli subsp. hispidula
intro: Echinochloa crus-galli subsp. hispidula, commonly known as green fortail, is a subspecies of grass belonging to the Poaceae family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Chinese and Ayurvedic practices, for its various medicinal properties. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as promote wound healing and improve overall health. Green fortail can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional remedies, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
